figures.caption: Figure 2: Relationship between AlpacaEval LC win rates and the hyperparameter α on UF-G. The semi-transparent area represents standard deviations.
Figure 3: Relationship between AlpacaEval LC win rates and the hyperparameter α on MIX-14K. The semi-transparent area represents standard deviations.
Figure 4: Training losses over steps for DDRO, KTO, and RDRO on Llama-8B with UF-G and α = 0.39. Note that the loss values are not directly comparable across methods since the objectives differ.
Figure 5: Gradient norms over steps for DDRO, KTO, and RDRO on Llama-8B with UF-G and α = 0.39.
Figure 6: Preferred log-ratios over steps for DDRO, KTO, and RDRO on Llama-8B with UF-G and α = 0.39.
Figure 7: Non-preferred log-ratios over steps for DDRO, KTO, and RDRO on Llama-8B with UF-G and α = 0.39.

Figure 1: (a) Comparing density ratio g∗(y|x) and relative density ratio r∗(y|x), where non-preferred data distribution p−(y|x) = 0.1 and the hyperparameter α = 0.5. Although g∗(y|x) diverges as p+(y|x) → 0, r∗(y|x) is bounded above by 1/α. (b) Our loss functions for preferred (blue) and non-preferred (orange) samples with α = 0.3. The loss for preferred samples is minimized when Tθ ≡ log pθ(y|x)− log pref(y|x) attains log(1/α).
